title: Hs Standards Test — Instrument Type Test
author: Peter Higgins
summary: Three synthetic test objects of known geometry — cylinder, sphere, cube — each built from pure CoDa compositions (D=27 carriers, T=41 timesteps). The projector renders each object correctly without fabricating structure. The instrument does not generate pattern where none exists, and does not suppress pattern where it is present.

methodology.normalization: per-run min-max applied to CLR values before polar projection
methodology.projection: polar polygon — each carrier maps to an angular spoke, CLR magnitude maps to radial distance
methodology.degenerate_detection: timestep flagged DEGEN when CLR spread < threshold (all carriers indistinguishable)
methodology.lock_detection: first and last timesteps where the projector resolves a non-degenerate polygon; offset is the CLR spread at that boundary

test_objects.label: Cylinder Standard
test_objects.construction: Uniform composition at every timestep: p_j = 1/D = 0.037037 for all j, all t. CLR transform maps all carriers to the same value. CLR spread = 0 everywhere (machine precision: |CLR| < 2e-15).
test_objects.expected_projection: No resolvable polygon at any timestep. All 41 timesteps degenerate. Projector should display DEGEN marker at every frame with no polygon drawn.
test_objects.observed_projection: DEGEN marker at all 41 timesteps. No polygon rendered at any timestep. No false structure introduced.
test_objects.pass_criterion: Zero false polygon renderings across all 41 timesteps.

test_objects.label: Sphere Standard
test_objects.construction: CLR spread follows a sin envelope: amplitude(t) = 0.8 * sin(pi * t / 40). At t=0 and t=40 amplitude=0, yielding uniform composition. Peak amplitude 0.800 at t=20 (midpoint equator). Each carrier perturbed by amplitude * cos(2*pi*j/D) before closure.
test_objects.expected_projection: Expanding circle from t=0 (degenerate pole) to t=20 (maximum radius equator), then contracting back to t=40 (degenerate pole). DEGEN markers at both poles. LOCK markers at first and last resolvable timesteps.
test_objects.observed_projection: DEGEN at t=0 and t=40. Lock acquisition at t=1 (CLR spread = 0.125110). Lock loss at t=39 (CLR spread = 0.125110). Polygon expands symmetrically from t=1 to t=20, contracts symmetrically from t=20 to t=39. Circle shape maintained throughout.

lock_acquisition.note: First timestep at which projector resolves a non-degenerate polygon. Offset from true zero is real instrument resolving boundary, not error.

lock_loss.note: Last timestep at which projector resolves a non-degenerate polygon. Symmetric with lock acquisition by construction.

test_objects.pass_criterion: Correct DEGEN at both poles, correct LOCK markers at t=1 and t=39, monotonic expansion and contraction of circle radius between boundaries.

test_objects.label: Cube Standard
test_objects.construction: Carriers split into 4 angular quadrants (6 carriers each, with 3 remainder carriers assigned to quadrant 3). Quadrants 0 and 2 (C01-C06, C13-C18) hold weight 2.0 before closure. Quadrants 1 and 3 (C07-C12, C19-C27) hold weight 0.5 before closure. After closure: high carriers p = 0.063492, low carriers p = 0.015873 (4:1 ratio). Composition is identical at all 41 timesteps.
test_objects.expected_projection: Square-like polygon (two high lobes, two low lobes at 90-degree offsets) constant through all 41 timesteps. Zero degenerate timesteps.
test_objects.observed_projection: Constant polygon with high lobes at C01-C06 and C13-C18 angular positions, low lobes at C07-C12 and C19-C27 positions. Shape identical at all timesteps. Zero DEGEN frames. No shape drift.
test_objects.pass_criterion: Non-degenerate polygon at all 41 timesteps, constant shape across time, correct 4:1 lobe structure.

root.build_standards.py: Python script that generates all three polar_stack JSON files from first principles
root.STD-standards_suite_projector.html: Combined projector showing all three test objects in a single instrument view
root.Hs-STD_JOURNAL.md: Master experiment journal — full methodology, observations, and conclusions

cylinder.STD-cylinder_polar_stack.json: Polar stack data — 27 carriers, 41 timesteps, uniform composition
cylinder.README.md: Cylinder test object specification and result

sphere.STD-sphere_polar_stack.json: Polar stack data — 27 carriers, 41 timesteps, sin-envelope CLR spread
sphere.README.md: Sphere test object specification and result

cube.STD-cube_polar_stack.json: Polar stack data — 27 carriers, 41 timesteps, alternating quadrant composition
cube.README.md: Cube test object specification and result

three_laws_compliance.law_1_no_fabrication: VERIFIED — cylinder produced zero false polygons; no structure was rendered where none existed in the data
three_laws_compliance.law_2_every_nibble_preserved: VERIFIED — all 27 carrier CLR values present at all 41 timesteps in each polar_stack JSON; no data suppressed
three_laws_compliance.law_3_confidence_stated: VERIFIED — DEGEN markers explicitly flag unresolvable timesteps; LOCK markers document resolving boundary

dut_principle: The data is always the Device Under Test. The projector is the measuring instrument. This test suite verifies that the instrument reads correctly when given inputs of known geometry.
reference: Aitchison, J. (1986). The Statistical Analysis of Compositional Data. Chapman and Hall.
